title: sida atherophora subsp. magnifica
benefits: Sida atherophora subsp. magnifica has many benefits, such as being a rich source of antioxidants, which help protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and heart disease. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The benefits of Sida atherophora subsp. magnifica also extend to its potential in reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and even supporting weight loss, making it a valuable addition to a healthy lifestyle. By incorporating this plant into their daily routine, individuals can experience improved overall well-being, increased energy levels, and a greater sense of vitality, ultimately enhancing their quality of life.
constituents: Sida atherophora subsp. magnifica has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, terpenoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which possess a wide range of medicinal properties. These constituents, including quercetin, kaempferol, and isorhapontigenin, exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, helping to protect against chronic diseases like cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. The presence of glycosides, like rutin and apigenin-7-O-glucoside, contributes to the plant's analgesic and anti-anaphylactic effects, while the alkaloids, such as sidaflavine and sidaflavinone, demonstrate potential in treating pain and inflammation. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Sida atherophora subsp. magnifica, individuals can benefit from improved cardiovascular health, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function, leading to a better quality of life.
side_effects: Sida atherophora subsp. magnifica can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting, due to its high toxicity level. Prolonged consumption can also lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as allergic reactions, resulting in skin irritation, itching, and respiratory issues. In severe cases, excessive consumption may cause cardiovascular problems, including high blood pressure and irregular heartbeat. It's essential to consult a medical professional before using this plant for medicinal purposes to avoid these adverse effects.
